Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. Datahäxan 102 upvotes, 8 comments
  2. Dolphin Progress Release 2603 152 upvotes, 15 comments
  3. Printf-Tac-Toe 71 upvotes, 6 comments
  4. WebPKI and You 75 upvotes, 9 comments
  5. Avoiding Trigonometry (2013) 117 upvotes, 24 comments
  6. Show HN: I built a tool that watches webpages and exposes changes as RSS 288 upvotes, 75 comments
  7. 1B identity records exposed in ID verification data leak 136 upvotes, 32 comments
  8. Temporal: The 9-year journey to fix time in JavaScript 742 upvotes, 237 comments
  9. Don't post generated/AI-edited comments. HN is for conversation between humans 3853 upvotes, 1441 comments
  10. Making WebAssembly a first-class language on the Web 613 upvotes, 224 comments
  11. High fidelity font synthesis for CJK languages 18 upvotes, 2 comments
  12. Tested: How Many Times Can a DVD±RW Be Rewritten? Methodology and Results 196 upvotes, 64 comments
  13. 3D-Knitting: The Ultimate Guide 151 upvotes, 51 comments
  14. US banks' exposure to private credit hits $300B 40 upvotes, 10 comments
  15. Show HN: s@: decentralized social networking over static sites 355 upvotes, 158 comments
  16. Show HN: Axe A 12MB binary that replaces your AI framework 12 upvotes, 2 comments
  17. ArcaOS 5.1.2 (based on OS/2 Warp 4.52) now available 16 upvotes, 4 comments
  18. Returning to Rails in 2026 240 upvotes, 148 comments
  19. SBCL: A Sanely-Bootstrappable Common Lisp (2008) [pdf] 83 upvotes, 44 comments
  20. NASA's DART spacecraft changed an asteroid's orbit around the sun 60 upvotes, 30 comments
  21. Reliable Software in the LLM Era 38 upvotes, 19 comments
  22. Big Data on the Cheapest MacBook 173 upvotes, 118 comments
  23. Show HN: We analyzed 1,573 Claude Code sessions to see how AI agents work 28 upvotes, 17 comments
  24. The purpose of Continuous Integration is to fail 9 upvotes, 3 comments
  25. I was interviewed by an AI bot for a job 375 upvotes, 372 comments
  26. Emacs internals: Tagged pointers vs. C++ std:variant and LLVM (Part 3) 7 upvotes, 2 comments
  27. Iranian Hacktivists Strike Medical Device Maker Stryker and Wiped Systems 28 upvotes, 21 comments
  28. The MacBook Neo 605 upvotes, 947 comments
  29. Show HN: I built an SDK that scrambles HTML so scrapers get garbage 6 upvotes, 5 comments
  30. Show HN: Calyx – Ghostty-Based macOS Terminal with Liquid Glass UI 7 upvotes, 15 comments

Last generated 10:30pm, 12 March 2026. Source here.
